f0648605-3d71-4236-beda-07422722dc51.title: Visit the Wikipedia page for 'Python (programming language)' and extract release year, creator, and current stable version

details.intent: Mandatory single-URL visit. Extract three facts directly from the page content (not from search snippets): (1) the year Python was first released, (2) the name of Python's creator, and (3) the current stable version number. Provide exact page-text citations for each fact.

target_facts.description: Year Python was first released (YYYY). Expected to be found in the infobox or lead paragraph under 'First appeared', 'First released', or similar.
Name of Python's creator (e.g., 'Guido van Rossum'). Expected to be found in the lead paragraph and/or infobox 'Designed by' / 'Developer(s)'.
Current stable version number as shown on the page (e.g., '3.10.4' or similar). Expected to be found in the infobox under 'Stable release' or in the lead.

details.extraction_plan: 1) Load the exact URL provided and read full page content. 2) Locate the infobox at the top-right of the article and extract values for 'First appeared'/'First released' (or similar), 'Designed by'/'Developer(s)' and 'Stable release'. 3) If a fact isn't in the infobox, scan the lead paragraphs and the 'History' or 'Version history' sections for explicit statements. 4) For each fact, copy the exact snippet(s) from the page (including surrounding text up to 240 characters to provide context), and record the character offsets (start/end) relative to the fetched page content if available. 5) Produce three concrete output fields (year_first_released, creator_name, current_stable_version) plus citation objects containing the exact snippet, the page URL, and the location on the page.

measurable_output_fields.creator_name: string (full name) extracted verbatim from page
measurable_output_fields.current_stable_version: string (version number) extracted verbatim from page

citations.snippet: string (<=240 chars) exact text from page containing the fact

verification_steps: Confirm each extracted fact appears verbatim on the page (infobox or lead).
If multiple candidate values appear (e.g., multiple version numbers), prefer the value labeled 'Stable release' and include the surrounding label text in the citation.
If the 'first released' field shows a full date (day/month/year), extract and report only the year but include the full date in the citation snippet and note the exact text.
Record the exact snippet(s) so the user can verify the source against the page.
details.rationale: User explicitly mandated a direct visit to this exact Wikipedia URL and requires citations from the page content. This node performs that required data-gathering step and produces verifiable evidence (snippets + exact URL) for the three requested facts.
details.note_on_vector_db: As per system rules, a semantic index will be queried automatically to surface any previously stored links/releases; however this node will still fetch and extract from the provided exact URL to satisfy the user's mandate.

details.intent: Post-visit verification and formatting step. Take the raw extractions from the visit node, confirm they match the target_facts, remove ambiguity, and prepare the final user-facing JSON with explicit citations (snippet + URL + location). If any fact is ambiguous or not found, record which fact failed and why.
details.expected_input: Output from the visit node containing the page content and extracted snippets for each target_fact.

outputs.verification_log: If any extraction was uncertain, a short note describing the ambiguity and the exact text quoted from the page.

verification_steps: Ensure each output field is present and populated from the visit output.
Check that the snippet includes the label (e.g., 'Stable release') when reporting version.
If the page gives a full date for 'first released', include the full date in citation and return the year in the final field.
If creator name appears in multiple forms (e.g., full name and short form), choose the full legal name as used in the lead and include the lead sentence in the citation.
details.rationale: Think node is used here only to verify and format the findings (per system rules, think nodes come after actions). This reduces user uncertainty by ensuring the extracted facts are accurate, unambiguous, and accompanied by clear citations.
